_comment: Income Protection (IP) Financial Underwriting Rules — IP-F-XXX prefixed rules for benefit period, waiting period, income replacement ratio, occupation hazard mapping, and multiple policy coordination.

metadata.description: Financial underwriting rules for Income Protection insurance covering benefit period selection, waiting period configuration, income replacement ratio limits, occupation class hazard mapping, and multiple policy coordination.

rules.name: benefit_period_rules
rules.description: Benefit period rules — determines the maximum duration for which IP benefits can be paid, including 2-year, 5-year, to-age-65, and to-age-70 options with associated risk loading.

2yr.notes: Short-term benefit period — lowest premium cost, limited coverage duration

5yr.notes: Medium-term benefit period — standard option for most applicants

to_age_65.notes: Long-term benefit to retirement age — highest claim exposure

to_age_70.notes: Extended benefit period — available for select occupations, highest premium

rules.underwriting_notes: Longer benefit periods significantly increase claim exposure. To-age-70 is restricted to professional occupations with low physical demands.

rules.name: waiting_period_rules
rules.description: Waiting period rules — defines the minimum period between disability onset and benefit commencement, with shorter waiting periods carrying higher premium loads.

30.notes: Short waiting period — highest premium, requires 6 months employment history

60.notes: Standard waiting period — balanced premium and coverage

90.notes: Recommended waiting period — standard baseline rating

180.notes: Long waiting period — lowest premium, requires substantial savings buffer

rules.underwriting_notes: Shorter waiting periods increase claim frequency. 30-day waiting periods may be declined for high-risk occupations. Minimum employment history verifies insurable interest.

rules.name: income_replacement_ratio
rules.description: Income replacement ratio — limits the percentage of pre-disability income that can be replaced by IP benefits, typically capped at 75% to maintain work incentive and prevent moral hazard.

parameters.notes: Replacement ratio decreases at higher income levels to maintain work incentive

rules.underwriting_notes: Income verification is mandatory. Declining slab rates at higher incomes reduce moral hazard. Approved income sources include salary, bonuses, commissions, and rental income.

rules.name: occupation_class_hazard_mapping
rules.description: Occupation class hazard mapping — maps occupation classes to standard IP hazard factors used in premium calculation and underwriting decisions.

class_1_professional.notes: Low hazard — professional occupations have lowest IP claim rates

class_3_blue_collar.notes: Moderate hazard — standard benchmark for IP pricing

class_4_heavy_manual.notes: High hazard — manual occupations with elevated injury risk

class_5_extreme_manual.notes: Very high hazard — extreme manual work, may require specialist underwriting

rules.underwriting_notes: Occupation class is the primary driver of IP pricing. Detailed occupational descriptions are required for borderline classifications.

rules.name: multiple_policy_coordination
rules.description: Multiple policy coordination — prevents over-insurance by coordinating benefits across all IP policies held by the applicant, ensuring total benefits do not exceed income replacement limits.

parameters.notes: Total IP benefits across all policies cannot exceed approved income replacement ratio. Existing policies are disclosed and benefits coordinated pro-rata.

rules.underwriting_notes: Applicants must disclose all existing IP policies. Non-disclosure is grounds for claim denial. Benefits are coordinated to prevent over-compensation.

rules.name: maximum_benefit_amount
rules.description: Maximum benefit amount — sets the absolute cap on monthly IP benefits regardless of income, based on regulatory limits and insurer capacity.

excess_benefit_process.notes: Benefits above standard cap require specialist underwriting and additional loading

hard_cap.notes: Absolute maximum monthly benefit — no exceptions

rules.underwriting_notes: Benefit caps protect against moral hazard and limit insurer exposure. Excess benefits require enhanced underwriting.

rules.name: premium_rating_factors
rules.description: Premium rating factors — calculates the final premium by applying all applicable loading factors including age, occupation, gender, health, and policy configuration.

gender_factor.notes: Female discount based on lower claim frequency in IP products

minimum_premium.notes: Minimum premium floor regardless of risk profile

rules.underwriting_notes: Premium is calculated by multiplying base rate by all applicable factors. Final premium is subject to minimum premium floor.

rules.name: coinsurance_requirements
rules.description: Co-insurance requirements — determines the applicant's required co-insurance (shared risk) percentage to maintain skin-in-the-game and reduce moral hazard.

standard_coinsurance.description: Applicant retains 25% of income risk — standard for all IP policies
standard_coinsurance.notes: 25% co-insurance is mandatory for all IP policies

reduced_coinsurance.notes: 10% co-insurance available for low-risk professional occupations aged 25-50

no_coinsurance.notes: Zero co-insurance is not permitted for IP products

rules.underwriting_notes: Co-insurance ensures the applicant has financial incentive to return to work. Standard 25% co-insurance applies to all policies.

rules.name: indexation_benefit_rules
rules.description: Indexation benefit rules — governs the inflation protection option that increases the benefit amount annually in line with CPI or a fixed percentage, subject to limits.

cpi_linked.notes: CPI-linked indexation — benefit increases with inflation up to 5% cap

fixed_percent.notes: Fixed percentage indexation — applicant selects increase rate

no_indexation.notes: No inflation protection — benefit amount remains fixed

cumulative_cap.notes: Total indexed increase cannot exceed 100% of original benefit

claims_inflation_protection.notes: Indexation continues to apply to benefits during claim period

rules.underwriting_notes: Indexation increases long-term claim exposure. CPI-linked indexation provides market-aligned protection but carries higher cost.

rules.name: benefit_excess_deductible
rules.description: Benefit excess/deductible rules — allows applicants to choose a benefit excess (waiting period extension after disability onset) to reduce premiums.

constraints.notes: Benefit excess is additional to the waiting period. Combined total determines time from disability to benefit payment.

rules.underwriting_notes: Benefit excess reduces claim frequency by shifting short-term claims to personal savings. Cannot exceed remaining waiting period.
